full_text:
**Meeting Notes 15042025**

**Topic:** TLS Segmentation Issues\
**Date:** 15.04.2025\
**Attendees:** Anniina, Ada, Hanna, Zhihan, Ziqi

**1. TLS Segmentation Strategy**

- **Mesmer vs. Stardist:**

 - Mesmer shows better performance with longer running times
 compared to Stardist.

 - Mesmer runs on small cropped tiles; additional resources are
 needed to apply it to full-slide images.

 - Tile stitching consideration: ensure proper handling of cells in
 overlapping regions.

 - Black areas (bubbles) can cause segmentation error -- better
 skipping and need to filter or avoid black tiles.

- **Benefits of Mesmer:**

 - Performs more accurately on thicker tissue sections.

 - This improved performance will benefit future projects,
 including proteopicks using thicker sections.

- **Optimization Plan:**

 - Ziqi will spend one week post-Easter to optimize and upgrade the
 Mesmer-based segmentation pipeline. Lina has scripts that could
 support this process.

- **Backup Plan:**

 - If whole-slide segmentation is not feasible, fallback will be to
 crop individual TLS regions and segment them using Mesmer
 separately.

**2. Tissue Section Thickness Considerations**

- **4 μm sections:**

 - Used in SPACE project, better but still suboptimal for
 segmentation of mature TLS structures.

- **5 μm sections (current study):**

ss Considerations**

- **4 μm sections:**

 - Used in SPACE project, better but still suboptimal for
 segmentation of mature TLS structures.

- **5 μm sections (current study):**

 - Chosen to collect more material for GeoMx analysis, causing
 segmentation issues in dense areas like TLSs.

- **General Note:**

 - Thinner sections are preferable for H&E staining.

 - Thicker sections are more suitable for RNA/protein profiling.

 - A future reporting system is applied to track section thickness
 (in μm) used per experiment.

**3. Action Points & Next Steps**

- By **next Friday**, Ziqi will assess feasibility of Mesmer WSI
 optimization and decide whether the current plan needs to be
 adjusted. Ada will assist Ziqi with all necessary existing materials
 on the meeting of next Tuesday 22th. Zhihan will also follow and
 assist with segmentation optimization.

- Hanna will contact:

 - **Oskari** for H&E-based scripts to identify lymphoid aggregates
 and TLSs, and try to generate the quantitative data.

 - **Joona** for biological thresholds and TLSs / Immune aggs
 definitions to help with classification.

 - Zhihan will ask **Anastasia** to check for normal/benign cases
 in the Oncosys-Ova cohort to obtain **normal omentum** samples
 as negative controls. We may get around 3-5 samples within our
 cohort.
